goal: Land session-scoped deduplication in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` — files already in `SessionInfo::files_in_context` are excluded from a candidate Vec via a new `pub async fn dedup_against_context(&self, id: &SessionId, candidates: Vec<PathBuf>) -> Vec<PathBuf>` method on `SessionManager`, plus a bulk-insert companion `pub async fn add_files_to_context(&self, id: &SessionId, files: &[PathBuf]) -> Option<()>`, with the dedup store implicitly cleared on session expiry via the existing `purge_expired` retain (no new state — reuses the existing `BTreeSet<PathBuf>` field per master-plan §6.3 line 666). Frozen acceptance test `session_manager::test_session_dedup` lands at MODULE ROOT of `session_manager.rs` per DEC-0007 (matching the existing `test_session_state_tracking` placement at line 439), covering 5 behaviours: (1) empty `files_in_context` returns candidates unchanged, (2) partial overlap filters out the in-context paths only, (3) full overlap returns an empty Vec, (4) calling against a non-existent / purged session returns candidates unchanged (session-expiry → dedup-store-cleared invariant), (5) bulk `add_files_to_context` accumulates across calls so subsequent dedup applies the union. Cite master-plan §5.2 line 459 ('Session dedup: don't return same code block twice in a session'), §6.3 line 666 ('1. Session dedup: remove results the agent already has (files_in_context)'), §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782 ('Session deduplication tracking') in the rustdoc + commit body.
plan_summary: Edit `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` (≈+150 LOC, single-file) to add (a) `pub async fn dedup_against_context(&self, id: &SessionId, candidates: Vec<PathBuf>) -> Vec<PathBuf>` immediately after the existing `add_file_to_context` at line 235 — read-locks `self.sessions`, looks up the session, returns `candidates.into_iter().filter(|p| !info.files_in_context.contains(p)).collect()`; on `None` (session purged/missing) returns `candidates` unchanged so the dedup-store-cleared-on-expiry invariant is structural; (b) `pub async fn add_files_to_context(&self, id: &SessionId, files: &[PathBuf]) -> Option<()>` adjacent to (a) — write-locks once, extends the BTreeSet via `info.files_in_context.extend(files.iter().cloned())`, returns `Some(())` on success and `None` if the session is missing; (c) frozen acceptance test `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_session_dedup()` at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007 — selector resolves to `ucil_daemon::session_manager::test_session_dedup`. The test body exercises 5 sub-assertions (empty context / partial overlap / full overlap / purged-session bypass / bulk-add accumulation) and uses `crate::test_support::env_guard()` per DEC-0011 since `create_session` spawns `git`. NO changes to `lib.rs` re-exports — `SessionManager` is already in the public surface (line 137-139); the new methods become reachable transitively. NO changes to `server.rs` MCP-tool wiring — production wiring of dedup into `search_code` / `find_references` is deferred to P2-W7-F06 per master-plan §5.2 line 459 → §6.3 line 666 staging (response-assembly is the fusion-layer's natural consumer). Pre-bake 3 mutation entries in `acceptance` (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons line 245): (M1) `dedup_against_context` body neutered to `candidates` (no filter) → AC11 sub-assertion 2 panics because filtered output equals input despite overlap; (M2) `dedup_against_context` body neutered to `Vec::new()` (drop everything) → AC11 sub-assertion 1 panics because empty-context test expects pass-through, not empty; (M3) `add_files_to_context` body neutered to `Some(())` (no insert) → AC11 sub-assertion 5 panics because bulk-add accumulation does not occur and dedup leaves all 3 candidates in place.
scope_in: Edit `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` adding `pub async fn dedup_against_context(&self, id: &SessionId, candidates: Vec<PathBuf>) -> Vec<PathBuf>` immediately after the existing `add_file_to_context` method (currently at line 235). Method body: `let sessions = self.sessions.read().await; match sessions.get(id) { Some(info) => candidates.into_iter().filter(|p| !info.files_in_context.contains(p)).collect(), None => candidates, }`. The session-missing branch returning `candidates` unchanged is the STRUCTURAL implementation of master-plan §5.2 line 459 'session-scoped dedup store is cleared on session expiry' — once `purge_expired` removes a session, future dedup calls become no-ops automatically. Rustdoc MUST cite master-plan §5.2 line 459 + §6.3 line 666 + §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782 verbatim, document the session-missing → pass-through invariant, and include a `# Examples` block (NOT `no_run` — the example uses only synchronous in-memory state via `SessionManager::new()` so a doctest can `tokio::runtime::Runtime::new` and call it).

Edit `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` adding `pub async fn add_files_to_context(&self, id: &SessionId, files: &[PathBuf]) -> Option<()>` adjacent to `dedup_against_context`. Body: `self.sessions.write().await.get_mut(id).map(|info| info.files_in_context.extend(files.iter().cloned()))`. The signature uses `&[PathBuf]` (NOT `Vec<PathBuf>`) so callers can pass either a `&Vec` or a slice without an allocation. Returns `None` if the session is missing — same shape as the existing `add_file_to_context`. Rustdoc cites the bulk-insert use-case (multi-result search returns N file paths in one shot — atomic insert under one write-lock vs N round-trips through `add_file_to_context`) and that `BTreeSet::extend` de-duplicates internally so calling twice with overlapping slices is a no-op on duplicates.

Add the frozen acceptance test `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] #[allow(clippy::await_holding_lock)] async fn test_session_dedup()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector `session_manager::test_session_dedup` resolves to `ucil_daemon::session_manager::test_session_dedup` — same placement convention as the existing `test_session_state_tracking` at line 439 (sibling at module root). The leading attribute block MUST be `#[cfg(test)]` then `#[tokio::test]` then `#[allow(clippy::await_holding_lock)]` with a comment `// DEC-0011: fence PATH mutations in watcher tests` — copy the existing pattern from line 432-441. Inside the test body, FIRST line is `let _g = crate::test_support::env_guard();` to fence concurrent PATH mutators per DEC-0011 (required because `create_session` spawns `git`).

The `test_session_dedup` body MUST exercise these 5 sub-assertions in order (one for each of the master-plan invariants): 
  **(SA1) Empty context → pass-through**: Create session via `sm.create_session(&repo).await.expect("session")` (use `std::env::current_dir().expect("cwd")` as repo root, matching line 442 / 517). Build `let candidates = vec![PathBuf::from("src/a.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/b.rs")]`. Call `let kept = sm.dedup_against_context(&id, candidates.clone()).await`. Assert `assert_eq!(kept, candidates, "empty files_in_context must return all candidates unchanged; got {:?}", kept)`. 
  **(SA2) Partial overlap → filtered subset**: `sm.add_file_to_context(&id, PathBuf::from("src/a.rs")).await.expect("session exists")`. Re-run `let kept2 = sm.dedup_against_context(&id, candidates.clone()).await`. Assert `assert_eq!(kept2, vec![PathBuf::from("src/b.rs")], "a.rs is in files_in_context — must be excluded; got {:?}", kept2)`. 
  **(SA3) Bulk add via `add_files_to_context` accumulates**: Build `let extras = vec![PathBuf::from("src/c.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/d.rs")]`. Call `sm.add_files_to_context(&id, &extras).await.expect("session exists")`. Re-run `let kept3 = sm.dedup_against_context(&id, vec![PathBuf::from("src/a.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/b.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/c.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/d.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/e.rs")]).await`. Assert `assert_eq!(kept3, vec![PathBuf::from("src/b.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/e.rs")], "only b.rs and e.rs survive after a/c/d are in context; got {:?}", kept3)`. This is the LOAD-BEARING assertion proving `add_files_to_context` actually inserts into `files_in_context` — if its body is mutated to `Some(())` no-op, this assertion fires. 
  **(SA4) Full overlap → empty Vec**: Re-run `let kept4 = sm.dedup_against_context(&id, vec![PathBuf::from("src/a.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/c.rs"), PathBuf::from("src/d.rs")]).await`. Assert `assert!(kept4.is_empty(), "all candidates already in context — must return empty Vec; got {:?}", kept4)`. 
  **(SA5) Session expiry → dedup pass-through**: Capture `let created_at = sm.get_session(&id).await.expect("session").created_at`. Set TTL to 1s: `sm.set_ttl(&id, 1).await.expect("session exists")`. Purge: `let removed = sm.purge_expired(created_at + 2).await; assert_eq!(removed, 1, "exactly one session expired")`. Confirm purge: `assert!(sm.get_session(&id).await.is_none(), "session must be gone")`. Re-run dedup: `let kept5 = sm.dedup_against_context(&id, candidates.clone()).await`. Assert `assert_eq!(kept5, candidates, "purged session → dedup-store-cleared → all candidates pass through; got {:?}", kept5)`. This is THE invariant for 'session-scoped dedup store is cleared on session expiry' from the feature description — verifies the structural design (no separate dedup state; the `BTreeSet` lives on `SessionInfo` and dies with the session).  
  Every `assert!`/`assert_eq!` MUST quote the actual observed value via `{:?}` so a mutation-induced failure prints actionable diagnostics (per WO-0042 lessons line 92, WO-0051 lessons line 405).

The 4 path strings in the test (`src/a.rs`, `src/b.rs`, `src/c.rs`, `src/d.rs`, `src/e.rs`) are arbitrary in-memory fixtures — they do NOT need to exist on disk. `BTreeSet<PathBuf>::contains` / `::extend` operate on path equality, not filesystem state. NO modification to `tests/fixtures/**` (forbidden_paths) and NO `tempfile::TempDir` — keep the test pure-in-memory like `test_session_state_tracking` does at line 464-484.

**No `lib.rs` re-export changes**. `SessionManager`, `SessionId`, `SessionInfo` are already in the public surface (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs:137-139`). The new methods `dedup_against_context` + `add_files_to_context` are accessible transitively via the existing `pub use session_manager::SessionManager;` line. AC07 verifies via `grep -nE 'pub use session_manager::' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` returning the existing line unchanged.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — for `dedup_against_context` mutation #1 (`candidates` pass-through), the body becomes `let _ = self; let _ = id; candidates` and the `unused_variable`/`needless_return` cascade is suppressed via `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::needless_pass_by_value, clippy::let_unit_value)]` on the function during the mutation step (un-mutate after); for mutation #2 (`Vec::new()` drop-all), the body becomes `let _ = self; let _ = id; let _ = candidates; Vec::new()`. For `add_files_to_context` mutation #3 (`Some(())` no-op), the body becomes `let _ = self; let _ = id; let _ = files; Some(())`. Document BOTH the literal `sed` AND the runtime-only variant in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC15 / AC16 / AC17) — verifier picks whichever produces a runtime-assertion failure rather than a compile-failure cascade.

**Pre-flight rustdoc grep before pushing** (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`): `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs | grep -v '^\s*///.*`' || true` should return zero matches OUTSIDE backticks for any new doc lines added by this WO. Phase 1 set `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` on this crate (`lib.rs:96`); use plain backticks (`SessionManager`, `BTreeSet`, etc.) NOT shorthand `[Foo]` intra-doc links.

**Operator-readable assertion messages** (per WO-0051 lessons line 405): every `assert_eq!` / `assert!` in `test_session_dedup` follows the shape `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", actual)` so a mutation-induced failure prints the panic message with the actual observed Vec. Pattern documented at WO-0051's `ripgrep_manifest_parses` precedent.

**Commit ladder — approximately 4 commits** (each ≤ ~50 LOC except the test commit, DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out): 
  (1) `feat(daemon): add SessionManager::dedup_against_context for session-scoped result filtering` (~30 LOC method + rustdoc), 
  (2) `feat(daemon): add SessionManager::add_files_to_context bulk insert` (~25 LOC method + rustdoc), 
  (3) `test(daemon): add session_manager::test_session_dedup at module root per DEC-0007` (~80 LOC; over the 50 LOC soft target — DEC-0005 carve-out: 5 sub-assertions form one coherent regression-guard for the feature contract; splitting would either (a) introduce a transient state where SA1+SA2 pass without SA5 verifying the expiry invariant, OR (b) duplicate the session-creation preamble across 5 commits), 
  (4) `docs(daemon): expand session_manager preamble citing P2-W7-F04 and master-plan §6.3` (~10-15 LOC rustdoc on the module-level `//!` block, preserving the existing P1-W4-F07 sentence and adding one new sentence naming the F04 method-pair + the master-plan §6.3 line 666 citation). 
  Each commit signed via the conventional `Phase: 2 / Feature: P2-W7-F04 / Work-order: WO-0052` trailer per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`.
scope_out: **No production wiring** of `dedup_against_context` into `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` MCP-tool handlers (`handle_search_code`, `handle_find_definition`, `handle_find_references`, etc.). Master-plan §6.3 line 666 places session dedup at response-assembly time (after fusion); the natural consumer is P2-W7-F06 (`search_code` MCP tool returning fused G2 results) per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1781-1783. F04's contract is the standalone method on SessionManager + the unit test verifying its semantics; F06 (sibling WO) wires it into search_code's post-fusion path. The CEQP `files_in_context` parameter (already declared in `server.rs:207`) is the agent-side input that callers will eventually merge into the session via `add_files_to_context` from the MCP handler — this WO does NOT do that merge. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` is in `forbidden_paths`.

**No changes to `SessionInfo`** struct shape (already has `files_in_context: BTreeSet<PathBuf>` at line 118). NO new field added; the dedup state IS the existing `files_in_context`. Adding a separate `dedup_seen` field would create two parallel sources of truth — the description explicitly says 'files already in `files_in_context`' (singular field).

**No changes to `purge_expired`** semantics or behaviour. The session-expiry → dedup-store-cleared invariant is structural — `purge_expired` removes the entire `SessionInfo` (including `files_in_context`) when it retains-out an expired session. The new `dedup_against_context` method observes this via the `None` branch of `sessions.get(id)`. AC09 verifies via SA5 (full purge cycle).

**No changes to `add_file_to_context`** (existing singular-insert at line 235). The new `add_files_to_context` is a sibling bulk-version, NOT a replacement. AC10 verifies the existing singular method still compiles + the existing `test_session_state_tracking` (line 466-474 calls `add_file_to_context` 3 times) still passes — regression guard from Phase 1 stays green.

**No new dependencies**. `BTreeSet`, `PathBuf`, `Vec` are std. `tokio::sync::RwLock` is already in `[dependencies]` (workspace-managed). `Cargo.toml` files MUST stay unchanged — AC12 verifies via `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- '*.toml'` returning empty.

**No new ADR**. DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC for the test commit specifically), DEC-0011 (`test_support::env_guard` PATH mutex for tests that spawn `git`) all cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. surprising `BTreeSet`-with-PathBuf hashing semantics, async-await deadlock on the read-then-write split between `dedup_against_context` and `add_files_to_context`), STOP and write an ADR before working around.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048 / 0050 / 0051, now 9 consecutive cleared).

**No changes to `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`** — F04 is single-crate (`ucil-daemon`), single-source-file (existing `session_manager.rs`). Same shape as WO-0048 (single-file `executor.rs` extension) but applied to `session_manager.rs`.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`, `server.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, `storage.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `startup.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `test_support.rs`, `lib.rs`, `main.rs`, or `bin/**`** — ONLY `session_manager.rs` changes. The `session_ttl.rs` arithmetic helpers (`compute_expires_at`, `is_expired`) are FROZEN per Phase 1; the test re-uses them via the existing `set_ttl` + `purge_expired` round-trip on `SessionManager`.
**No `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `commented-out assertions`, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

**No mock of the `SessionManager`'s internal `RwLock<HashMap<SessionId, SessionInfo>>`** — the `SessionManager` IS UCIL-owned state; mocking its internal map would defeat the purpose of the test. Tests use the real `SessionManager::new()` exactly like `test_session_state_tracking` does at line 443.
**No `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

AC15: **Mutation M1 (literal sed)** — apply `sed -i 's|candidates.into_iter().filter(|p| !info.files_in_context.contains(p)).collect()|candidates|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` → re-run AC04 → expect FAILURE at SA2 panic line (panic message: 'a.rs is in files_in_context — must be excluded; got ["src/a.rs", "src/b.rs"]'). **Runtime-only variant**: replace the entire body of `dedup_against_context` with `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::needless_pass_by_value, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = id; candidates }` — same expected fail-line. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` → AC04 green again.

AC16: **Mutation M2 (literal sed)** — apply `sed -i 's|candidates.into_iter().filter(|p| !info.files_in_context.contains(p)).collect()|Vec::new()|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` → re-run AC04 → expect FAILURE at SA1 panic line (panic message: 'empty files_in_context must return all candidates unchanged; got []'). **Runtime-only variant**: replace body with `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = id; let _ = candidates; Vec::new() }`. Restore: `git checkout`.

AC17: **Mutation M3 (literal sed)** — apply `sed -i 's|self.sessions.write().await.get_mut(id).map(|info| info.files_in_context.extend(files.iter().cloned()))|Some(())|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` → re-run AC04 → expect FAILURE at SA3 panic line (panic message: 'only b.rs and e.rs survive after a/c/d are in context; got ["src/b.rs", "src/c.rs", "src/d.rs", "src/e.rs"]'). **Runtime-only variant**: replace body with `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = id; let _ = files; Some(()) }`. Restore: `git checkout`.

AC18: Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble in `session_manager.rs` mentions `dedup_against_context` + cites master-plan §6.3 line 666 — verified via `grep -E 'dedup_against_context.*§6\.3|§6\.3.*dedup_against_context|line 666' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` returning at least 1 match. Documentation sign-post per WO-0045/0048 lessons (load-bearing preamble discipline).
